title: woodsia macrochlaena
intro: Woodsia macrochlaena, also known as the giant woodsia or maidenhair fern, is a species of fern native to North America and Asia. It is a medicinal plant used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Woodsia macrochlaena include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and aid in the treatment of various skin conditions, such as eczema and acne. Woodsia macrochlaena can be prepared as an herbal tea or infusion, and in some cultures, it is also used in traditional remedies, such as salves and poultices, to promote healing and reduce pain.
